sidebar.title: Corterm
sidebar.noSessionsHint: Tap to set up your first session

login.title: Corterm
login.subtitle: Sign in to continue
login.errorPhone: Please enter a valid 11-digit phone number

home.title: Native Features
home.subtitle: Native Capabilities Catalog
home.messagesDesc: Toast, Snackbar, and combined message entry points.
home.notificationsDesc: In-app notifications and pending navigation demos.
home.hapticsDesc: Click / heavy and other native haptic feedback.
home.photosDesc: Access the system photo gallery and preview images in the Web layer.
home.cameraDesc: Launch the system camera to take photos, automatically returning and previewing them.
home.videoDesc: Select or record videos and play them back in the Web layer.
home.bridgeDesc: JS calling C#, C# pushing to JS, text and binary examples.
home.preferencesDesc: View native Preferences keys and values known to the template.
home.themeDesc: Manage light / dark / system theme independently.
home.settingsDesc: Support entry, sharing, terms, and app info.

messages.title: Messages
messages.cardContent: Native message entry points for various message styles, easily extensible for more.
messages.initialDetail: Tap any entry below to trigger a native message.
messages.shortToastMsg: This is a short Toast message from C#
messages.longToastDesc: Suitable for more complete status messages.
messages.longToastMsg: This is a longer-lasting Toast for showing a more complete description.
messages.dismissToastResult: Requested dismissal of the current Toast.
messages.longSnackbarMsg: This is a long Snackbar, suitable for displaying longer descriptions or guidance.
messages.cancelSnackbarDesc: Demonstrates action button style and interaction.
messages.cancelSnackbarMsg: Snackbar displayed with a cancel button on the right.
messages.persistentSnackbarDesc: Suitable for guidance messages that require user attention.
messages.persistentSnackbarMsg: This is a persistent Snackbar. Use the close button or the entry below to dismiss.
messages.dismissSnackbarResult: Requested dismissal of the current Snackbar.
messages.comboResult: Toast triggered with click haptic feedback appended.

notifications.title: Notifications
notifications.cardSubtitle: In-app notifications and pending navigation examples
notifications.cardContent: Common in-app notification patterns. You can add local or push notification capabilities here later.
notifications.initialDetail: This template provides in-app notifications and pending navigation notification examples.
notifications.inAppNotifyDesc: Uses Snackbar as a lightweight notification feedback.
notifications.inAppNotifyMsg: This is an in-app notification alert
notifications.highPriorityDesc: Haptic feedback + longer notification with button.
notifications.highPriorityMsg: High priority notification: Your attention is needed.
notifications.highPriorityResult: High priority notification triggered: heavy haptic + long Snackbar.
notifications.writeNavDesc: Simulate notification tap navigating to the Bridge page.
notifications.writeNavResult: Bridge page written to pending navigation. You can simulate the notification tap navigation.
notifications.writeMsgNavDesc: Simulate notification tap navigating to the Messages page.
notifications.writeMsgNavResult: Messages page written to pending navigation.
notifications.readNavDesc: Reading will consume and clear the current pending navigation.

haptics.title: Haptics
haptics.cardSubtitle: Click / heavy and other interaction feedback
haptics.cardContent: Great for button confirmation, long-press feedback, and micro-interactions for key actions.
haptics.initialDetail: Test different native haptic feedback types here.
haptics.heavyDesc: More pronounced, suitable for confirmation actions.
haptics.doubleClickResult: Double-click rhythm triggered: click then click.
haptics.heavyClickDesc: Suitable for emphasizing a "confirmation complete" feeling.
haptics.heavyClickResult: Heavy then click combined haptic triggered.
haptics.hapticToastDesc: Common for successful submission or completed action scenarios.
haptics.hapticToastResult: Heavy haptic triggered with a Toast displayed.

photos.title: Photos
photos.cardSubtitle: Access system gallery and read metadata
photos.cardContent: Currently returns filename, type, and size. You can extend to preview, upload, or crop later.
photos.initialDetail: The most recent photo selection result will appear here.
photos.toastPickDesc: Give the user lightweight feedback immediately after a successful selection.
photos.cacheActionDesc: Save the most recent photo selection result to native Preferences.
photos.readCacheDesc: Verify the photo result was successfully saved to native storage.
photos.noPreviewDetail: After selecting a photo, it will be displayed here.
photos.note: If null is returned, the user likely cancelled the selection. On success, the photo is copied to the app cache for direct preview.

camera.title: Camera
camera.cardSubtitle: Launch system camera and return metadata
camera.initialDetail: The most recent photo capture result will appear here.
camera.captureDesc: Capture a photo and pass file metadata back to the Web layer.
camera.cacheActionDesc: Save the most recent capture result to native storage.
camera.readCacheDesc: Verify the camera result was successfully written to native Preferences.
camera.noPreviewDetail: After capturing a photo, the most recent image will be displayed here.
camera.note: The system may request camera permission on first use. Captured photos are copied to the cache directory so the Web layer can preview them.

video.title: Video
video.cardSubtitle: Select or record videos and preview directly in the Web layer.
video.initialDetail: The most recent video selection or capture result will appear here.
video.pickVideoDesc: After selection, copy to app cache and provide preview.
video.captureVideo: Open system camera to record video
video.captureVideoDesc: Demonstrates video capture return and preview flow.
video.cacheActionDesc: Write the most recent video result to native Preferences.
video.readCacheDesc: Verify the video result was successfully written to native storage.
video.noPreviewDetail: After selecting or recording a video, the player will be displayed here.
video.note: Video files are typically larger. This template copies them to the cache directory for preview.

bridge.title: JS and C# Interaction
bridge.cardSubtitle: Invocation, push, text, and binary channels
bridge.cardContent: Focused on verifying JS calling C#, C# pushing to JS, and bidirectional binary messages.
bridge.initialDetail: The most recent JS <-> C# call result will appear here.
bridge.initialIncomingDetail: The most recent C# -> JS text or binary message will appear here.
bridge.streamLinkDesc: JS sends start/stop commands, C# continuously pushes binary chunks to JS for display.
bridge.appInfoDesc: App name, package name, support email, etc.
bridge.capabilitiesDesc: View which native capabilities the current host exposes.
bridge.helloDesc: Simplest bridge call: invoke HelloAsync, returns world.
bridge.greetDesc: Pass in a name + language object, returns a multilingual greeting struct.
bridge.kvTestDesc: Verify the full JS -> C# -> Preferences -> JS chain.
bridge.textEchoDesc: Call C# via InvokeDotNet and get back a structured text result.
bridge.binaryEchoDesc: Encode a Uint8Array and send to C#, then receive the verification result back.
bridge.rawText: JS raw sends text to C#
bridge.rawTextDesc: Bypasses InvokeDotNet; C# listens for raw messages and responds directly.
bridge.rawTextResult: Text sent to C# via raw message:
bridge.rawBinary: JS raw sends binary to C#
bridge.rawBinaryDesc: Demonstrates JS sending a binary payload to C# and waiting for a C# response.
bridge.rawBinaryResult: Binary sent to C# via raw message.
bridge.pushTextDesc: After clicking, C# initiates the message; the JS page will receive and display it.
bridge.pushTextResult: Requested C# to push a text message to JS.
bridge.pushBinaryDesc: Have C# generate a byte array and send it to the JS side for display.
bridge.pushBinaryResult: Requested C# to push a binary message to JS.
bridge.incomingNote: This section displays C# messages pushed to JS, or C# responses to JS raw messages.

bridgeStream.title: Streaming Binary Bridge
bridgeStream.cardSubtitle: This page demonstrates "JS sends control commands, C# continuously sends binary data to JS".
bridgeStream.commandDesc: The three buttons below will have JS send a start command to C#.
bridgeStream.initialDetail: Click a preset button below. JS will send a start command to C#, which will then continuously push binary chunks here.
bridgeStream.initialCmdDetail: The most recent streaming control command sent from JS to C# will be recorded here.
bridgeStream.noDataDesc: After starting a stream, binary chunks pushed by C# will be appended here continuously.

preferences.title: Preferences
preferences.cardSubtitle: Display known native key-value cache from the template.
preferences.cardContent: Since MAUI Preferences does not support full enumeration, this shows only the known keys used by the template.
preferences.initialDetail: The most recent Preferences operation result will appear here.
preferences.refreshDesc: Re-fetch current known key-values from native Preferences.
preferences.note: Clearing only removes a single key and does not affect Web-side state in localStorage.

theme.title: Theme
theme.cardSubtitle: Standalone theme entry, separate from settings.
theme.cardContent: Switching modes here simultaneously updates the Ionic theme and MAUI native status bar color.

settings.title: Settings
settings.emailBody: Hi team, I want to discuss Corterm.
settings.supportDesc: The settings page now only contains real settings: sharing, support, and essential app info.
settings.linksDesc: These entries are well-suited for the About / Settings pages of all future apps.
settings.logoutConfirmMessage: Are you sure you want to sign out?

sessions.title: Sessions
sessions.noWorkers: No workers available. Please install and start a Worker first.

activate.title: Activate Worker
activate.codeFormatError: Activation code format should be XXXX-YYYY
activate.codeInvalid: Activation code is invalid or expired. Please check the code displayed in the terminal and try again.
activate.tokenExpired: Login expired. Please log in again and retry.
activate.activateFailed: Activation failed ({{status}}). Please try again later.
activate.networkError: Network error. Please check your connection and try again.
activate.successDesc: Worker has successfully connected to Gateway. You can now go back and create a Session.
activate.inputDesc: Run cortex login in the terminal, then enter the XXXX-YYYY code displayed on screen.

components.mediaPreviewEmptyDetail: After selecting or capturing, the most recent media preview will appear here.
components.mediaCached: - Copied to app cache for preview

catalog.title: UI Components
catalog.subtitle: Ionic interactive component demos
catalog.description: Below are interactive demos for all Ionic UI components. Tap to view detailed usage.

modal.title: Modal
modal.description: A Modal is a panel that overlays the current page, suitable for forms and details.
modal.name: Name

popover.title: Popover
popover.description: A Popover is a small popup attached to a trigger element, commonly used for quick menus.

actionSheet.title: Action Sheet
actionSheet.description: An Action Sheet slides up a set of action buttons from the bottom, ideal for confirmations.

alert.title: Alert
alert.description: Alerts display informational messages or request user confirmation and input.
alert.basicMessage: This is a basic Alert dialog.
alert.confirmMessage: Are you sure you want to proceed?

loading.title: Loading
loading.description: Loading is a full-screen or local loading indicator used while waiting for operations to complete.

toast.title: Toast
toast.description: Toast is a lightweight message notification that briefly appears at the top or bottom.
toast.warningMessage: Warning: please pay attention to this notice.

accordion.title: Accordion
accordion.description: Accordion provides collapsible content panels supporting single and multiple selection modes.
accordion.item1Content: Ionic is an open-source mobile app development framework that uses Web technologies to build cross-platform apps.
accordion.item2Content: React is a JavaScript library developed by Facebook for building user interfaces.
accordion.item3Content: .NET MAUI is Microsoft's cross-platform UI framework that runs on multiple platforms with a single codebase.

fab.title: FAB
fab.description: FAB (Floating Action Button) is an action button that floats above content.

tabs.title: Tabs
tabs.description: Tabs provide bottom or top tab navigation, suitable for switching between multiple views.
tabs.tab1Content: This is the Home content area.
tabs.tab2Content: This is the Search content area.
tabs.tab3Content: This is the Profile content area.

nav.title: Nav
nav.description: Nav provides stack-based page navigation with push and pop support.

grid.title: Grid
grid.description: Grid provides a 12-column responsive grid layout system.

segment.title: Segment
segment.description: Segment provides a segmented control for switching content between options.
segment.photosContent: This is the Photos content area.
segment.videosContent: This is the Videos content area.
segment.audioContent: This is the Audio content area.

refresher.title: Refresher
refresher.description: Refresher provides pull-to-refresh functionality with customizable state text.

infiniteScroll.title: Infinite Scroll
infiniteScroll.description: Infinite Scroll automatically loads more data when scrolling to the bottom.

searchbar.title: Searchbar
searchbar.description: Searchbar provides an input field with a search icon, supporting real-time filtering.

itemSliding.title: Item Sliding
itemSliding.description: Item Sliding supports swiping left or right on list items to reveal action buttons.

reorder.title: Reorder
reorder.description: Reorder allows users to rearrange list items by dragging.

input.title: Input
input.description: Input provides various types of text fields including text, email, password, and more.

textarea.title: Textarea
textarea.description: Textarea provides a multi-line text input supporting auto-grow and character count.
textarea.readonlyContent: This is read-only text content that cannot be edited.

select.title: Select
select.description: Select provides a dropdown selector supporting single and multiple selection modes.

radioCheckbox.title: Radio & Checkbox
radioCheckbox.description: Radio is for single selection, Checkbox is for multiple selection.

datetime.title: Datetime
datetime.description: Datetime provides date and time pickers.

rangeToggle.title: Range & Toggle
rangeToggle.description: Range provides a slider control, Toggle provides a switch control.

chip.title: Chip
chip.description: Chips are compact tag elements supporting add, remove, and icons.

progress.title: Progress
progress.description: Progress shows operation progress with determinate and indeterminate modes.

avatarThumbnail.title: Avatar & Thumbnail
avatarThumbnail.description: Avatar displays user avatars, Thumbnail displays image thumbnails.

spinner.title: Spinner
spinner.description: Spinner provides various styles of loading animation indicators.

catalogItems.modal: Overlay panel for forms and detail views
catalogItems.popover: Small popup menu attached to an element
catalogItems.infiniteScroll: Auto-load more on scroll to bottom
catalogItems.itemSliding: Swipe list items to reveal actions
catalogItems.input: Various types of text input fields
